findings.title: macOS wizard choices are bypassed by tests
findings.suggested_fix: Add focused wizard tests that stub gum_choose and exercise all three branches in _step9_macos_defaults: recommended should serialize the intended category set, custom should serialize only selected labels, and skip/empty selection should write backup=false with no categories.

findings.title: macOS backup regression test checks existence, not exported preference content
findings.suggested_fix: Make the test deterministic by prepending stub macOS commands for defaults, plutil, scutil, networksetup, and related report commands. Feed known plist/json fixtures, then assert the exported keyboard-shortcut plist preserves both enabled and disabled shortcut state, text replacements are removed, and default-app duti rows contain the expected bundle, handler, and role values.

findings.title: Generated restore behavior is only grepped, not executed
findings.suggested_fix: Add a restore-side regression that runs the generated install.sh or phase_final in dry-run/fixture mode with stub defaults, duti, killall, and find inputs. Assert defaults import receives NSGlobalDomain and normal domains correctly, and duti is invoked with the tab-separated rule fields from macos/default-apps.duti.

residual_risks: I did not execute the test suite because this persona review was scoped read-only; findings are based on the scoped diff and test inspection.
Some macOS domains may be absent on a given host, so even passing current tests may not prove behavior across supported macOS versions.
testing_gaps: No coverage for recommended/custom/skip macOS wizard selection serialization.
No deterministic fixture coverage for keyboard shortcut enabled/disabled state or duti LSHandlers parsing.
No restore execution test proving generated defaults import and duti commands receive correct arguments.
